message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Monterey Crossings (Chick-fil-A, Dickey's Barbecue Pit, Pokehana) (Palm Desert)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Cluster-level: Chick-fil-A ~210 Yelp reviews / ~638 Google reviews; Pokehana (Hwy 111 PD location, not Monterey Crossings pad) 229 Yelp reviews; Dickey's Palm Desert location reviews limited/dispersed [UNVERIFIED whether a Dickey's pad operates specifically at Monterey Crossings vs. nearby corridor]
- Sentiment: Mixed to mostly positive — Chick-fil-A 3.4-3.9 stars (mixed: service inconsistency, long waits flagged); Pokehana 4.4 stars (positive on freshness and service); Dickey's generally positive in regional listings
- Longevity: Development cluster: Chick-fil-A opened July 14, 2022; Monterey Crossings specific plan approved May 2017; cluster still building out with 130-room hotel pending [UNVERIFIED exact opening dates for Dickey's and Pokehana pads at Monterey Crossings specifically]
- Credentials: []
- Appears in queries: Individual chain tenants surface for branded queries ('Chick-fil-A Palm Desert', 'Pokehana Palm Desert', 'Dickey's Palm Springs area'). The cluster name 'Monterey Crossings' surfaces in commercial real estate queries (LoopNet, Hanley Investment, KDC Construction) and trade press (Palm Springs Life Vision, GPS Business Insider) — not in consumer dining queries. Does NOT surface for 'best restaurants Coachella Valley', 'fine dining Palm Desert', 'date night Rancho Mirage'. May surface for 'fast food near I-10 Palm Desert' but as individual chain pins, not as a cluster.
- Site structured data: Monterey Crossings has no consumer-facing site — only developer/leasing pages (KDC Construction, Hanley Investment, LoopNet) and adwarchitects portfolio page. Tenant sites: Chick-fil-A and Dickey's run on robust corporate domains with location structured data. Pokehana's site (pokehana.com) lacks visible JSON-LD/schema.org markup; hours and locations are present but not machine-structured.
- Visibility gap score: 2
- Convertibility tier: anchor-reference

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the web search results, here's what I found about Monterey Crossings in Palm Desert:

## Project Overview
Monterey Crossing is a 17-acre retail development in the City of Palm Desert. The Monterey Crossing Specific Plan covers the approximate 17.79-acre parcel located on the North-East Corner of Monterey Avenue and Dinah Shore Drive, and the specific plan facilitates the development of a commercial shopping center containing retail, restaurant, drive-through restaurant, hotel and other auto-oriented service uses.

## KDC Construction Involvement
The freeway-adjacent project includes single-story commercial space for multiple tenants and was scheduled to be completed in the fall of 2021.

## Tenants
The retail center includes the valley's only Chick-Fil-A, The Habit, Spectrum and more. Additionally, a multi-tenant retail pad features Jersey Mike's Subs, a rapidly growing national sandwich chain.

## Site Plan Resources
The City of Palm Desert's planning department provides access to the Monterey Crossing Specific Plan document at their zoning information page (palmdesert.gov/departments/planning/zoning), where you can review the detailed site plan.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.text: Critical finding: The KDC project page lists tenants as Chick-Fil-A, The Habit, Spectrum, and Jersey Mike's — NOT Dickey's Barbecue Pit or Pokehana. The actual Dickey's location is at 5200 Ramon Rd in Palm Springs, not at Monterey Crossings. Let me verify more thoroughly.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about the Monterey Crossing Specific Plan in Palm Desert:

On May 11, 2017, the Palm Desert City Council approved the Specific Plan and other documents for the Monterey Crossing project, an 18-acre mixed-commercial center at the northeast corner of Monterey Avenue and Dinah Shore Drive.

The specific plan for Monterey Crossing, approved in 2017, is an 18-acre mixed-use commercial center that includes drive-thru restaurants, shops, and retail buildings, a car dealership, and up to a four-story hotel.

The Monterey Crossing Specific Plan covers the approximate 17.79‐acre parcel located on the North‐East Corner of Monterey Avenue and Dinah Shore Drive and will facilitate the development of a commercial shopping center containing retail, restaurant, drive-through restaurant, hotel and other auto-oriented service uses.

To find this information, you would need to visit the individual location pages or use the restaurant locator feature on their website.

content.text: Interesting — there's a Dickey's in Rancho Mirage, not Palm Desert. Let me verify the Chick-fil-A opening date and the Rancho Mirage Dickey's address.

# Chick-fil-A I-10 & Monterey Ave Opening Details

The restaurant serves the Coachella Valley area and operates from 6:30 a.m. to 10 p.m., Monday through Saturday, with dine-in, drive-thru, and mobile ordering options available.

content.text:
Clear confirmation: Dickey's Barbecue Pit is at 42452 Bob Hope Drive, Rancho Mirage (Rancho Las Palmas Shopping Center) — NOT at Monterey Crossings, NOT even in Palm Desert. The brief's wording acknowledges this uncertainty but the framing still conflates these as cluster tenants.

Chick-fil-A opening date (July 14, 2022) and Monterey Crossings approval (May 11, 2017) both confirmed.

confirms: Chick-fil-A at Monterey Crossings opened July 14, 2022 at 73070 Dinah Shore Drive, Palm Desert (confirmed via Chick-fil-A press room and KESQ).
Monterey Crossings Specific Plan was approved by Palm Desert City Council on May 11, 2017, covering ~17.79 acres at NE corner of Monterey Ave and Dinah Shore Drive.
Cluster still building out — plan includes a ~130-room four-story hotel (TownePlace Suites by Marriott reportedly moving through planning).
Pokehana Palm Desert (73405 Hwy 111, Ste 104) has 229 Yelp reviews — confirmed via Yelp listing title.
Monterey Crossings has no consumer-facing site — only KDC Construction, Hanley Investment, LoopNet, and Fountainhead developer pages surface in searches.
contradicts: Claim implies/groups Dickey's Barbecue Pit as part of the Monterey Crossings cluster. There is NO Dickey's at Monterey Crossings. The actual valley Dickey's is at 42452 Bob Hope Drive, Rancho Mirage (Rancho Las Palmas Shopping Center) — a different city and different shopping center entirely. A second Palm Springs reference (5200 Ramon Rd) also exists but is not at Monterey Crossings. The brief should remove Dickey's from the Monterey Crossings tenant set.

Claim implies Pokehana could be associated with Monterey Crossings (and the brief does flag this as 'not Monterey Crossings pad'). Independent confirmation: Pokehana's only Palm Desert location is at 73405 Hwy 111, Ste 104 — a Highway 111 storefront, not the Monterey Ave/Dinah Shore Drive pad. The 229 Yelp reviews belong to that Hwy 111 location and should not be counted toward Monterey Crossings.

Implied tenant mix: independent sources (KDC, search summaries) list Monterey Crossings tenants as Chick-fil-A, The Habit Burger Grill, Spectrum, and Jersey Mike's — not Dickey's or Pokehana. The brief's framing of a 'Chick-fil-A / Dickey's / Pokehana' cluster mischaracterizes the actual tenant roster.
flagged_as_unverified: Yelp/Google review counts for the Chick-fil-A (~210 Yelp / ~638 Google) — not independently verified in this pass; plausible order of magnitude but not confirmed.
Star ratings (Chick-fil-A 3.4-3.9, Pokehana 4.4, Dickey's 'generally positive') — not verified in this pass.
Exact opening dates for Dickey's Rancho Mirage and Pokehana Hwy 111 — neither is at Monterey Crossings, so the underlying question is moot, but specific open dates were not independently pinned down.
Pokehana site (pokehana.com) lacking JSON-LD/schema.org markup — not independently inspected this pass.
Whether the 130-room hotel is still pending vs. opened — search results suggest TownePlace Suites planning is ongoing but current status not pinned down.
input.notes: The biggest issue is the entity framing itself. Monterey Crossings is correctly identified as a 2017-approved 17.79-acre development at Monterey Ave & Dinah Shore Drive with Chick-fil-A as a confirmed July 2022 anchor tenant. But Dickey's Barbecue Pit is NOT a Monterey Crossings tenant — it's in Rancho Mirage at the Rancho Las Palmas Shopping Center. Pokehana is NOT a Monterey Crossings tenant either — it's a Hwy 111 storefront. Independent sources name the actual Monterey Crossings tenants as Chick-fil-A, The Habit, Spectrum, and Jersey Mike's. The brief should either (a) rescope the entity to just Chick-fil-A at Monterey Crossings plus the actual co-tenants (Habit/Spectrum/Jersey Mike's), or (b) treat Dickey's Rancho Mirage and Pokehana Hwy 111 as separate entities with their own briefs. Credentials section is correctly empty — none of these chains carry James Beard, Michelin, or comparable independent culinary recognition (verified by absence in earlier audit work and the obviously chain/QSR nature of the tenants).
